response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack developer with strong software craftsmanship credentials, but is a poor match for this role's core requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ programming; the resume shows only ~7 months of professional C/C++ work (National Instruments, 2014-2015) plus a thesis project. Experience is concentrated in web/product engineering (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than large-scale cloud compute infrastructure, and there is no evidence of formal technical leadership over cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: High-performance systems exposure at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling authorship, and active mentoring via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded, including Kubernetes and Docker deployment
concerns: Falls far short of the 8-year C++ requirement, with only a brief C/C++ stint a decade ago
No production cloud compute platform or infrastructure ownership; background is product/web engineering
No demonstrated cross-team technical leadership role managing priorities, deadlines, and alignment at platform scale
